beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check the day ahead.
beats.reasoning: Mason wakes at a reasonable Friday hour, moderately energized (50/100 yesterday) but not rushed. Morning routine is quick—he's got the day ahead in mind.

beats.activity: Drive to the tattoo studio in Georgetown.
beats.reasoning: Fremont studio (per profile) is actually in Georgetown per the workplace note. Drive from Maple Leaf is ~20 min in light Friday morning traffic; budget 35 min to be safe.

beats.activity: Open studio, sketch designs, consult with early clients, set up for the day.
beats.reasoning: Mason values the quiet morning studio time for design work and client conversations—he mentioned this yesterday as grounding. Friday morning is typically less chaotic.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a sandwich and coffee nearby, scout the warm weather.
beats.reasoning: Mason already likes Salumi from yesterday's decompression walk. Perfect spot on a warm Friday; he can eat outdoors, shoot some film, enjoy the 77F weather without being crowded (Pride is downtown, not here yet).
Friday afternoon is typically busy for a tattoo artist; Mason can stack 3–4 solid sessions. He's in a good headspace after lunch and the morning design work.

beats.activity: Drive from Georgetown to Capitol Hill to catch Pride street fair before peak crowds.
beats.reasoning: Pride kickoff starts at 5pm downtown; Broadway and Pike closures mean traffic is building. Mason leaves work early to beat the rush and catch the festival while still catching decent light for photos. He's social and this fits his vibe.

beats.activity: Walk through Capitol Hill Pride street fair, shoot film photos, enjoy the energy and festive vibe.
beats.reasoning: Mason carries a film camera everywhere and values good light and close friends/community energy over big anonymous crowds. Early evening at Pride lets him enjoy the aesthetic and atmosphere without the 10pm crush. Warm breezy weather is perfect for this.

beats.activity: Head to a quiet Capitol Hill spot for an early dinner.
beats.reasoning: Rather than fight the street fair crowds for food, Mason grabs dinner at a quieter vantage—maybe a nearby deli or café, or brings food to the park. Warm evening, good light for photos, and he can decompress before heading home.
By 8:15pm, the peak Pride crowds and road closures are in full swing. Mason heads home to avoid the chaos; it's a 25–35 min drive depending on Capitol Hill traffic, budgeted at 45 min.

beats.activity: Wind down at home: review film photos from the day, play a game of chess online, relax.
beats.reasoning: Mason loves chess and photography; after a full day of work and social time, he decompresses at home with low-key activities. Moderate energy from yesterday means he's not pushing late into the night.
8 hours of sleep, ending well before 6am the next calendar day, as required. Mason gets solid rest after a full Friday.

diary:
Woke up to perfect weather—one of those mornings that makes you grateful to live here. The studio was steady all day; back-to-back sessions meant I barely noticed the hours slipping by. Grabbed lunch at Salumi, just stood outside for a minute soaking in the sun with my coffee.

Left work early on impulse. Pride kickoff was happening, and I hadn't been downtown for something like that in ages. Capitol Hill was already buzzing when I got there—so much color, so much life. I brought my film camera and just walked, shooting candids of people celebrating. There's something about that energy that reminds you why you stay in a city like this.

Had dinner alone at Volunteer Park, watching the light change. Drove home before rush hour got crazy. Now I'm back, looking through the negatives I shot, playing chess online. Tired but good. The kind of day that feels balanced.
thought_process: I decided to skip the evening rush and head to Pride on a whim—normally I'd stay at the studio through close, but the weather was too good and something about the day felt like it needed that. Work was solid, but predictable. When I heard the street fair was kicking off, I realized I've been in a bit of a routine rut lately. Grabbing my film camera felt right; it's something I used to do more. The energy downtown was infectious, and it shifted my whole mood from tired-and-grinding to actually present. I chose Volunteer Park for dinner because I needed quiet after the crowds, not more noise. Coming home early gave me space to actually process the day instead of collapsing into bed. Sometimes the best days are the ones where you interrupt your own pattern.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
